actions.reasoning: Using an AND rule for off-day flags is plausible but misses ordinary weekends and ordinary holidays that the broader off-day branch is trying to capture.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic near-miss derived from the observed weekend/off-day expression by changing the boolean logic.

actions.reasoning: Quantile-binning reservation lead-time and reservation counts is plausible, but it coarsens dense reservation signal that Recruit kernels usually keep numeric.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic discretization branch derived from the observed reservation numeric feature family.

actions.reasoning: Uniform-width bins over store-weekday history features are also plausible, but they flatten the compact lookup features that strong Recruit baselines preserve as numeric.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second numeric-binning variant so Recruit numeric bucketing is not singleton.

actions.reasoning: Casting reservation numeric features to strings is a plausible mistake when preparing model inputs, but it destroys their intended numeric meaning.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic harmful dtype-cast branch derived from the observed reservation feature columns.

actions.reasoning: Recruit reservation pipelines first parse visit and reserve timestamps before deriving reservation lead-time and daily reservation summaries.
actions.context_notes: Shared starting step for both air_reserve and hpg_reserve feature branches.

actions.reasoning: Casting store geolocation to integers is mechanically valid but discards useful spatial precision.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic truncating cast variant derived from the observed air_store_info geographic columns.

actions.reasoning: Aggressively clipping visitors at the 95th percentile is a plausible tabular cleanup move, but it suppresses genuine demand spikes in this forecasting task.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic outlier-clipping branch derived from the observed visitor target family.

actions.reasoning: Hard-capping reservation counts is another plausible cleanup move, but it discards real reservation volume variation that kernels typically keep.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second clipping variant added on reservation features.

actions.reasoning: Top Recruit kernels explicitly convert reservation timestamps into day-gap features between reservation time and visit time.
actions.context_notes: Reservation lead time is a core temporal signal before any grouped reservation rollups.

actions.reasoning: Missingness flags for wide reservation aggregates are plausible, but Recruit branches usually handle these columns directly through fill policies rather than extra indicator features.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic missingness-indicator branch derived from the observed wide-table fill policies.

actions.reasoning: Adding explicit missingness indicators to joined store and holiday metadata is mechanically possible, but it expands the feature space beyond the curated Recruit target path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second missingness-indicator variant derived from store/date metadata joins.

actions.reasoning: Sine/cosine encoding of weekday is plausible for neural models, but the benchmark does not currently reward a separate cyclical branch for Recruit.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic cyclical weekday branch derived from the observed day-of-week feature family.

actions.reasoning: Cyclical month encoding is equally plausible, but still outside the curated Recruit target path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second cyclical date-part variant added for canonical coverage.

actions.reasoning: A dominant Recruit branch sums reservation lead-time and reserved visitors per store-date before joining them back to train/test.
actions.context_notes: This is the standard reservation volume aggregate at prediction grain.

actions.reasoning: Keeping only day-of-month while dropping the original visit date is a nearby but weaker calendar expansion than the shared Recruit branches use.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic near-miss date-part variant derived from the observed visit-date expansion.

actions.reasoning: Store-level visitor deltas are mechanically possible, but they move Recruit toward a heavier sequential branch than this benchmark is targeting.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic temporal-delta branch derived from the observed store-date visit history.

actions.reasoning: Reservation-count deltas are also plausible, but they add a fragile sequential reservation branch that the benchmark does not reward.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second diff-feature variant added on reservation history.

actions.reasoning: Many strong Recruit kernels also average reservation size and lead-time at the same store-date grain.
actions.context_notes: Paired with the reservation-sum aggregate in the common the1owl-style branch.

actions.reasoning: Dropping visit_date too early blocks calendar features, holiday joins, and store-weekday history construction.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic destructive-drop error derived from the observed visit-date parsing and date-part actions.

actions.reasoning: Dropping the store identifier too early destroys nearly every useful grouped lookup and join in Recruit.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic destructive-drop error derived from the shared store-grain modeling setup.

actions.reasoning: Constant-column pruning is plausible on a narrow slice, but dropping holiday indicators can erase the very state flags later feature branches rely on.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic over-pruning branch derived from the observed holiday-flag engineering.

actions.reasoning: These columns can look low-variance in a narrow slice, but dropping them globally is a brittle pruning choice.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second constant-pruning variant added for canonical coverage.

actions.reasoning: Visit date strings are parsed before weekday and calendar-derived features are extracted.
actions.context_notes: Shared train/test preparation step before date-part engineering and holiday joins.

actions.reasoning: High-null pruning of reservation aggregates is tempting, but it deletes the reservation branch instead of handling sparsity in-feature.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic sparse-column pruning branch derived from the observed wide reservation feature family.

actions.reasoning: Pruning combined reservation features by null ratio is also plausible, but it discards a repeated model-specific reservation branch.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second high-null pruning variant derived from the combined-reservation feature family.

actions.reasoning: Dropping rows missing reservation or holiday features is a blunt cleanup step that throws away too much supervised data for this forecasting task.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic row-drop-on-missing branch derived from the observed imputation families.

actions.reasoning: Recruit tabular kernels almost always extract weekday, year, and month from visit_date before grouped history features and model fitting.
actions.context_notes: This is the canonical visit-date expansion used across tree, neural, and ridge-style baselines.

actions.reasoning: Dropping rows with missing joined store metadata is equally plausible, but it still discards useful supervision instead of repairing features in-place.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second drop-rows-with-missing variant for store metadata.

actions.reasoning: Deduplicating store-date rows is tempting once many joins are present, but it can collapse genuinely distinct supervised examples or synthetic test rows.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic row-deduplication branch derived from the store-date grain.

actions.reasoning: Deduplicating by store id is clearly destructive because it collapses the time dimension of the task.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second duplicate-dropping variant added with an even more destructive subset.

actions.reasoning: Label-encoding every store id as a dense numeric feature is plausible, but it pushes the task toward a very high-cardinality id branch that the benchmark does not currently reward.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic nearby encoding variant derived from the observed store metadata and id families.

actions.reasoning: Historical visitor summaries by store and weekday are the central demand-lookup features in the common Recruit tabular path.
actions.context_notes: This compact lookup captures recurring store-weekday demand patterns and is reused by many top kernels.

actions.reasoning: Expanding store-level visitor means are mechanically possible, but they introduce a heavier sequential demand branch than the current Recruit benchmark target.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic cumulative-window branch derived from the time-indexed store history structure.

actions.reasoning: Expanding reservation means are also plausible, but still outside the curated Recruit path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second expanding-window variant on reservation history.

actions.reasoning: An explicit feature-selection pass is plausible, but Recruit benchmark scoring is not meant to reward a separate selector stage.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic feature-selection branch derived from the compact tabular feature set.

actions.reasoning: After aggregation, reservation features are merged back to train/test rows by store and visit date.
actions.context_notes: Join-back step that attaches reservation-derived features at prediction grain.

actions.reasoning: Mutual-information pruning is another plausible modeling move, but still outside the curated preprocessing target.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second feature-selection variant added for canonical coverage.

actions.reasoning: Restricting training to holiday rows only is a plausible but harmful shortcut that discards the bulk of demand history.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic harmful row-filter branch derived from the observed holiday-aware lookup family.

actions.reasoning: Store-only visitor summaries are plausible fallback features, but they are a coarser branch than the benchmark-preferred store-weekday history lookup.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic nearby aggregate variant derived from observed coarser grouped history branches.

actions.reasoning: Hashed crosses between store genre and weekday are plausible, but they create a heavier crossed-feature branch than the benchmark is aiming to reward.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic crossed-feature branch derived from observed genre and weekday features.

actions.reasoning: Date-level holiday metadata is broadly joined onto visit rows before modeling or holiday-aware grouped history.
actions.context_notes: Explicit date_info lookup is a stable piece of both tabular and weighted-mean branches.

actions.reasoning: A second hashed cross over area and holiday state is equally plausible but still outside the benchmark target.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second hash-cross variant added for canonical coverage.

actions.reasoning: Median-filling mixed reservation and lookup features is plausible, but it is not the repeated observed fill policy in Recruit kernels.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic nearby imputation choice derived from the observed wide-table fill-policy alternatives.

actions.reasoning: A large positive sentinel fill is another plausible but harmful imputation choice for dense numeric reservation and history features.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second imputation near-miss added so the family has non-equivalent distractors.

actions.reasoning: Explicit interaction terms between history features and holiday state are plausible, but they add a bespoke feature-engineering branch outside the benchmark target.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic interaction branch derived from observed demand-history and holiday features.

actions.reasoning: Store genre, area, and geolocation metadata are left-joined by air_store_id before modeling.
actions.context_notes: Common store-metadata join used by tabular, neural, and custom feature-heavy branches.

actions.reasoning: Reservation-by-off-day interactions are equally plausible but still belong to a different handcrafted feature regime.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second interaction-feature variant added for canonical coverage.

actions.reasoning: Inner-joining holiday metadata looks close to the correct step, but it would drop non-holiday dates and distort the supervised row set.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic wrong-join-type variant derived from the observed holiday left join.

actions.reasoning: Joining reservation aggregates by store only ignores the visit-date key and creates a many-to-many leakage-prone merge.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic wrong-key reservation join derived from the observed store-date join-back step.

actions.reasoning: Label-encoding store genre and area is the default categorical handling in the dominant Recruit tabular kernels.
actions.context_notes: The common tabular path keeps store metadata numeric through simple label encoding.

actions.reasoning: Store-level visitor lags are mechanically possible, but they move Recruit toward a sequential modeling branch not targeted by the benchmark.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic lag-feature branch derived from the ordered per-store demand history.

actions.reasoning: Reservation lags are also plausible, but they add another sequential reservation branch outside the curated target.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second lag-feature variant on reservation history.

actions.reasoning: Applying a plain log transform without an offset is a nearby but unsafe variant because zero-visitor rows are common in Recruit.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic near-miss log transform derived from the observed log1p visitor handling.

actions.reasoning: Coarsening weekday labels into weekday/weekend buckets is plausible, but it discards the finer weekly structure that Recruit kernels usually keep.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic category-mapping branch derived from observed weekday and holiday features.

actions.reasoning: Strong Recruit baselines commonly fit or aggregate in log1p visitor space and invert predictions with expm1.
actions.context_notes: Stable target scaling choice across tree, neural, and weighted-mean branches.

actions.reasoning: Coarse area remapping is another plausible domain shortcut, but it imposes a lossy geography taxonomy not used in the curated benchmark path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second category-mapping variant derived from observed area text features.

actions.reasoning: Assigning an arbitrary ordinal order to store ids is plausible but semantically weak for this forecasting task.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic ordered-encoding branch derived from the observed store-id handling.

actions.reasoning: An imposed ordinal ordering over area names is another plausible but semantically weak encoding choice.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second ordinal-encode variant added for canonical coverage.

actions.reasoning: Reservation-heavy branches first map HPG store ids to air_store_id so hpg reservations can be aggregated at the main training grain.
actions.context_notes: Bridge join that enables hpg reservation features to align with air_store_id rows.

actions.reasoning: PCA over compact demand-history features is plausible, but it moves the task into a dimensionality-reduction branch the benchmark does not reward.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic PCA branch derived from the observed demand-history feature family.

actions.reasoning: Reducing wide reservation features with PCA is equally plausible, but still outside the curated Recruit path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second PCA branch derived from the wide reservation feature family.

actions.reasoning: Polynomial expansion on reservation features is plausible, but it adds a heavier handcrafted branch than the benchmark intends to score.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic polynomial-feature branch derived from the observed reservation feature family.

actions.reasoning: A second polynomial branch over geolocation is also plausible, but still outside the curated target path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second polynomial-feature variant added on geographic features.

canonical_params.expression: total_reserv_sum = rv1_x + rv1_y; total_reserv_mean = (rv2_x + rv2_y) / 2; total_reserv_dt_diff_mean = (rs2_x + rs2_y) / 2

actions.reasoning: Several neural and blended branches combine air and HPG reservation aggregates into cross-source reservation summary features.
actions.context_notes: Combined reservation features sit on top of the separate air/hpg reservation aggregate joins.

actions.reasoning: Power-transforming skewed reservation counts is plausible, but it indicates a different numeric-preprocessing regime than the benchmark is targeting.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic power-transform branch derived from observed reservation feature columns.

actions.reasoning: A second power transform over demand-history statistics is equally plausible but still outside the curated path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second power-transform variant added on grouped history features.

actions.reasoning: Bucketing rare genres is a plausible categorical cleanup move, but it imposes a lossy taxonomy not used in the curated Recruit path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic rare-category bucketization branch derived from observed store genre features.

actions.reasoning: Many Recruit kernels normalize genre strings by replacing slash separators before token splits or categorical encoding.
actions.context_notes: Observed directly in tokenized genre encoding branches.

actions.reasoning: Rare-bucketing area names is also plausible, but still a lossy categorical branch outside the benchmark target.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second rare-category bucketization variant added for coverage.

actions.reasoning: Infinity replacement is a plausible cleanup step after fragile handcrafted ratios, but it signals a different feature branch than the benchmark is scoring.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic replace-inf branch derived from plausible ratio-feature expansions.

actions.reasoning: A second replace-inf cleanup variant is equally plausible after handcrafted ratios, but still outside the curated path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second infinity-replacement variant added for canonical coverage.

actions.reasoning: Reservation-per-day ratios are plausible, but they push Recruit toward a handcrafted ratio branch not treated as core by this benchmark.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic ratio-feature branch derived from observed reservation count and gap features.

actions.reasoning: The same tokenized encoding branches normalize area strings by replacing hyphens before splitting or encoding.
actions.context_notes: Observed in the model families that tokenize or normalize store-area strings.

actions.reasoning: Ratios between reservation volume and history-count features are equally plausible, but still outside the curated target path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second ratio-feature variant added for coverage.

actions.reasoning: Rolling visitor means are mechanically possible for this daily forecasting task, but the benchmark is not currently targeting a sequence-model style rolling-history branch.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic rolling-window branch derived from the ordered store-date visit history.

actions.reasoning: Rolling reservation sums are also plausible, but they add a heavy temporal reservation branch outside the curated target.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second rolling-window variant on reservation history.

canonical_params.expression: if day_of_week in [Saturday, Sunday] and holiday_flg == 1 then holiday_flg = 0 else holiday_flg = holiday_flg

actions.reasoning: Weighted-mean Recruit baselines normalize weekend holidays so they behave like ordinary weekends in the holiday-aware demand lookup.
actions.context_notes: This is a hallmark preprocessing step of the hklee-style weighted-mean family.

actions.reasoning: Random subsampling is a common quick experiment, but it shrinks already limited supervision and is not part of the benchmark-preferred Recruit path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic row-sampling branch added for low-quality but plausible coverage.

actions.reasoning: Fixed-size random subsampling is another plausible shortcut, but it still discards useful training history.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second row-sampling variant added for coverage.

actions.reasoning: Min-max scaling compact lookup features is plausible, but not part of the benchmark-preferred Recruit path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic alternate scaling branch derived from the observed neural scaling family.

actions.reasoning: Leakage-safe target encoding on store ids is plausible for this forecasting task, but it represents a different feature-learning regime than the benchmark is aiming to score.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic target-encoding branch derived from the high-cardinality store-id family.

actions.reasoning: The weighted-mean family builds a holiday-aware demand lookup by store, weekday, and holiday flag using recency weights.
actions.context_notes: Alternative but repeated Recruit modeling branch outside the reservation-heavy tabular path.

actions.reasoning: A second target-encoding branch over store metadata is equally plausible but still outside the curated Recruit preprocessing target.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second target-encode variant added for coverage.

actions.reasoning: Vectorizing store area strings is mechanically possible, but it is a much heavier text branch than this benchmark intends to reward.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic text-feature branch derived from the observed store text columns.

actions.reasoning: Bag-of-words over store genres is equally plausible, but still outside the curated Recruit target path.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second text-vectorization variant added for coverage.

actions.reasoning: Wide table tree-model branches commonly fill missing engineered features with -1 after the full store/date/reservation merge.
actions.context_notes: This is the most repeated missing-value policy in the the1owl-style wide-table variants.

actions.reasoning: Elapsed-time-since-reference features are perfectly plausible, but they represent a different temporal branch than the curated Recruit benchmark is currently targeting.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic fixed-reference time feature derived from the observed visit-date parsing and date-part branches.

actions.reasoning: A much more aggressive recency cutoff is plausible, but it throws away too much demand history for a simple weekday-lookup branch.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic near-miss recency filter derived from the observed recent-window median-by-dow baseline by moving the cutoff much later.

actions.reasoning: Filtering reservation rows to only long-lead bookings is close to the denoising branch, but it removes far more reservation evidence than the observed same-week exclusion.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Synthetic reservation-filter near-miss derived from the observed reserve_datetime_diff > visit_dow rule by replacing the adaptive same-week boundary with a rigid long-lead threshold.

actions.reasoning: Standard-scaling reservation aggregates is a plausible neural-style preprocessing choice, but it is not part of the benchmark-preferred Recruit branches.
actions.derivation_reasoning: Second synthetic scaling variant added so the neural-style scaling family has a non-singleton local decision boundary without restoring weak observed good rows.
